response.justification: Candidate has relevant geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map, plus low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary skill, and the candidate's C/C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments back in 2014-2015 plus an undergraduate thesis. The dominant stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no Rust experience and no direct navigation/routing pipeline work. Worth a short screening call to probe systems depth and willingness to ramp on C++, but unlikely to meet the bar for a senior IC on this team.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map collaborations
Low-latency distributed systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services)
Strong Linux, testing discipline (TDD), and polyglot systems mindset
concerns: Minimal recent modern C++ experience and no Rust; primary stack is TypeScript and DLang
No demonstrated navigation guidance or routing-pipeline domain experience
Senior title but core systems-programming depth required for tiling work is unclear from resume
